message:
build: support in-place database builds and fix Vercel deploy configuration

## scripts/build-web-db.ts
- Add path resolution using Node's `resolve()` to normalize source and
  output paths before comparison
- Add check to skip the file copy operation when source and output paths
  resolve to the same file (in-place build)
- Log "Using in-place database at <path>..." when skipping copy
- This enables more efficient CI builds where the database is already in
  the correct location, avoiding unnecessary I/O operations

The previous implementation would always copy the database, which:
1. Wasted time/bandwidth in CI when the file was already in place
2. Could cause issues if source and output were the same file (attempting
   to copy a file onto itself)

## vercel.json
- Change --source argument from '../../phage.db' (root directory) to
  'public/phage.db' (already in web package's public folder)
- This works with the new in-place build support since the database is
  pre-positioned in packages/web/public/ during the Vercel build process
- Eliminates the need to reference files outside the web package directory
- The database is placed in public/ so it can be served as a static asset
  and fetched by sql.js in the browser

This change streamlines the Vercel deployment by recognizing that the
database file is already positioned correctly, avoiding a redundant copy
step and simplifying the build command path references.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1,6 +1,6 @@
 {
   "$schema": "https://openapi.vercel.sh/vercel.json",
-  "buildCommand": "cd packages/web && bun install && bun run ../../scripts/build-web-db.ts --source ../../phage.db --output public && bun run build",
+  "buildCommand": "cd packages/web && bun install && bun run ../../scripts/build-web-db.ts --source public/phage.db --output public && bun run build",
   "outputDirectory": "packages/web/dist",
   "installCommand": "bun install",
   "framework": null,
